title: The Hot Towel

jokes.text: Try not to wear shorts. It's not all that attractive to look at for five hours.
jokes.setup: Larry giving unsolicited travel advice to a stranger
jokes.punchline: Criticizing someone's appearance choices on a plane
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry - inserting himself into someone else's business with unsolicited criticism. Good character work.

jokes.text: I'm comfortable in pajamas, but I don't wear pajamas on a plane. I like to sing. I like to whistle. I like to play the bongos on my leg. I like to imitate horses... But I don't do it, okay? Because there's somebody sitting next to me.
jokes.setup: Passenger defending his right to wear shorts on a plane
jokes.punchline: Escalating list of absurd behaviors he restrains himself from doing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from reasonable to absurd. The horse imitation detail makes it memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: Yeah, they're grotesque.
jokes.setup: Asked if he's never seen a man's legs before
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment that men's legs are grotesque
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry bluntness. Economy of language with maximum impact.

jokes.text: Are you ready for a hot towel?
jokes.setup: Larry's heated argument about appropriate plane attire
jokes.punchline: Flight attendant's perfectly timed offer of hot towel service
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and ironic interruption of the argument. Sets up the episode's central incident.

jokes.text: She must've put it in the microwave. Don't you think? I mean, have you ever seen anything like this is your life?
jokes.setup: Larry received an overly hot towel
jokes.punchline: His dramatic overreaction to what's clearly a minor burn
jokes.explanation: Larry's tendency to catastrophize minor incidents. Good character work but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I bet you I could sue 'em. Don't you think? Do I have a case? Tell me. Would you testify for me?
jokes.setup: Larry's minor burn from hot towel
jokes.punchline: Immediately considering litigation and recruiting witnesses
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry litigiousness over trivial matters. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Yeah, it would be hard, in good conscience, could recommend a high-value settlement for that burn.
jokes.setup: Larry asking about suing over the hot towel burn
jokes.punchline: Doctor's dry medical assessment deflating Larry's lawsuit dreams
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man response that grounds Larry's absurdity in reality.

jokes.text: I'd love to get your home phone number just in case of an emergency or something else with the hand. Who knows? Something comes up, it's nice to have.
jokes.setup: Larry asking for doctor's contact info for legitimate medical follow-up
jokes.punchline: The inappropriate intimacy and persistence of the request
jokes.explanation: Larry's boundary-crossing behavior perfectly in character. Sets up future callbacks.

jokes.text: Why does he have the privilege of talking to you and not me? I'm your patient.
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining the answering service system
jokes.punchline: Larry's indignation at being treated like a regular patient
jokes.explanation: Larry's sense of entitlement and special treatment expectations are perfectly captured.

jokes.text: I don't want to be in your wallet. I want you in my wallet.
jokes.setup: Larry saying he likes having his doctor's number in his wallet
jokes.punchline: Doctor's inadvertently suggestive response
jokes.explanation: Excellent accidental double entendre with perfect economy. The awkwardness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I just came from the doctor. I've got two months to live. It's terrible.
jokes.setup: Larry running into ex-girlfriend Mary Jane
jokes.punchline: Fake terminal diagnosis to gain sympathy
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that's immediately undercut by Larry's explanation. Great character moment.

jokes.text: Yeah, I'm trying to get women to take pity on me, so they'll sleep with me, you know. That's all. So I just tell everybody that.
jokes.setup: Mary Jane's shocked reaction to his 'terminal diagnosis'
jokes.punchline: Larry casually revealing his manipulative strategy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry honesty about his dishonesty. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You regret breaking up with me though. You're still... You're still having nightmares about that?
jokes.setup: Reconnecting with ex-girlfriend after years
jokes.punchline: Larry's delusional confidence about her regret
jokes.explanation: Larry's narcissistic assumption that she's been pining for him. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Christian slater? He's a friend of yours? Yeah, tell him I said hi.
jokes.setup: Mary Jane mentioning Christian Slater will be at the party
jokes.punchline: Larry pretending to know a celebrity he clearly doesn't know
jokes.explanation: Classic social awkwardness - pretending familiarity with celebrity to impress.

jokes.text: I've got the number of every woman I've ever gone out with in my life. Believe me.
jokes.setup: Mary Jane suggesting he doesn't have her number
jokes.punchline: Larry's oddly proud admission of keeping all ex-girlfriends' numbers
jokes.explanation: Reveals Larry's attachment to past relationships in a slightly creepy way.

jokes.text: I don't know why she went out with me. I don't know. That's a good question. A big bowl of out of your league. Way out of my league.
jokes.setup: Jeff asking how Mary Jane looked
jokes.punchline: Larry's self-aware assessment of being outclassed
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating humor that shows rare self-awareness from Larry.

jokes.text: Maybe she's got the desperation this time. Hopefully. It's a lot easier to work with their desperation than mine.
jokes.setup: Discussing why Mary Jane asked him out
jokes.punchline: Larry's cynical but honest take on relationship power dynamics
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest and insightful observation about dating dynamics delivered with perfect Larry cynicism.

jokes.text: I'm a lefty unhooker. I can't unhook righty. It's from the car, I think, when you park. You're making out in the driver's seat, you lean over, it's your left hand that's doing the unhooking.
jokes.setup: Larry worried about unhooking bras with his injured hand
jokes.punchline: Detailed analysis of bra-unhooking handedness based on car geography
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific and relatable observation that most viewers never considered. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: A $300 gift certificate to matteo's restaurant. Have you been there? It's supposed to be great. Are you sure you wanna give that much?
jokes.setup: Larry presenting his expensive gift
jokes.punchline: The hosts' shock at his unusually generous gift
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the callback about Larry's historically terrible gifts.

jokes.text: What was the last gift? Both: 'The freak book.' 'The freak book.'
jokes.setup: Discussion of Larry's gift-giving history
jokes.punchline: Synchronized revelation of his previous terrible gift choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing with both saying it simultaneously. Establishes Larry's gift-giving reputation.

jokes.text: You're really going to town on that caviar. It's the best caviar they could possibly get. It's unbelievable. What do you think I'm gonna do, pass this up?
jokes.setup: Larry observing someone eating caviar at the party
jokes.punchline: Christian's unapologetic gluttony defense
jokes.explanation: Sets up the caviar etiquette lesson that follows. Good character conflict.

jokes.text: I think you're going over your allotment a little bit. No?
jokes.setup: Larry continuing to criticize caviar consumption
jokes.punchline: Treating party food like rationed resources
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry logic - applying formal rules to social situations

jokes.text: We have unwritten laws in this society. If I'm talking to somebody and they said their friend has cancer, I go 'oh. Oh. Oh.' Even though I don't know the person, I go 'oh.' You know, it's an unwritten law.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining social etiquette to Christian about caviar
jokes.punchline: His elaborate analogy about cancer sympathy as parallel to caviar sharing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Larry logic connecting disparate social rules. Classic overthinking of social situations.

jokes.text: What you should be doing here is you take a little bit, then you step away for 20 minutes, see what kind of action there is. If nobody's taking any, maybe take a little bit more, step away again. So forth and so on.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining proper caviar etiquette
jokes.punchline: His absurdly detailed strategic approach to party food consumption
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Larry's obsession with social rules. The military precision is hilarious.

jokes.text: Christian slater was eating gobfuls of it. I couldn't even look at it.
jokes.setup: Mary Jane asking Larry about eating all the caviar
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately throwing Christian under the bus
jokes.explanation: Larry's instant deflection and use of 'gobfuls' is perfect. Sets up future conflict.

jokes.text: as our gift to you, is gonna sing a special song. That's a gift? Are you kidding? How is that a gift?
jokes.setup: Susie announcing her daughter will sing as their gift
jokes.punchline: Larry's immediate incredulous reaction to this non-gift
jokes.explanation: Larry's blunt assessment of what constitutes a gift. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: All right. Okay. All right. Okay. That's good. That's good. Very good, very good! Okay, that was wonderful. What are you doing?
jokes.setup: Sammy singing at the party
jokes.punchline: Larry awkwardly interrupting mid-song thinking it's over
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe moment. The awkward interruption and Larry's confusion create maximum discomfort.

jokes.text: You ruined the whole gift! That was our gift for god's sake!
jokes.setup: Larry interrupting Sammy's song
jokes.punchline: Susie's outrage over the ruined 'gift'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the conflict. Reinforces the absurdity of the singing being their gift.

jokes.text: Thanks. Thanks a lot. Thank you. What? What did she say? She said that you told her that I helped myself to too much caviar.
jokes.setup: Christian confronting Larry after the party
jokes.punchline: Larry's betrayal by throwing Christian under the bus coming back to bite him
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Larry's earlier deflection. Consequences catching up with him.

jokes.text: Fat boy!
jokes.setup: Larry accidentally calling the wrong number
jokes.punchline: Stranger immediately insulting Larry
jokes.explanation: Random insult from wrong number is absurd but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: No no, I didn't call. How can you say you didn't call?! You did call. I didn't call purposefully. It was an accident. An accident call is still a call.
jokes.setup: Larry accidentally calling the doctor he promised not to call
jokes.punchline: The philosophical debate about whether accidental calls count as calls
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic debate. The doctor's ironclad reasoning against Larry's sophistry is perfect.

jokes.text: You know, I don't have an hourglass set up here, but it's the longest wrong number I've ever had in my life.
jokes.setup: Larry claiming it's just a wrong number
jokes.punchline: Doctor's sarcastic observation about the call length
jokes.explanation: Great comeback with specific visual detail that highlights Larry's behavior

jokes.text: I'm changing light bulbs and cleaning the cat's litter box. What do you do at home? Does anybody ask you what you do... Don't ask me what I do at home.
jokes.setup: Larry asking what the doctor is doing at home
jokes.punchline: Doctor's increasingly frustrated listing of mundane activities
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the doctor's frustration with Larry's inappropriate curiosity.

jokes.text: You don't like food. Every time we've gone out, you're like 'i don't like this food.' No, I didn't like your food. Your food... Come on, you were the worst cook in the world.
jokes.setup: Larry criticizing the restaurant food
jokes.punchline: Escalating into mutual food-related insults from their relationship
jokes.explanation: Good relationship history comedy showing why they broke up. Natural conflict.

jokes.text: You don't like food. Every time we've gone out, you're like 'i don't like this food.'
jokes.setup: Larry criticizing the restaurant food
jokes.punchline: Mary Jane's exasperated observation about Larry's food complaints
jokes.explanation: Good character insight that explains their past relationship problems

jokes.text: I'm planning on a big move later and I think I might hurt my chances if I keep criticizing your food. You're right. Yeah. A big big move. Is that what this is? Yeah, exactly. I'm babe ruth calling the shot.
jokes.setup: Larry realizing he should stop criticizing to improve his chances
jokes.punchline: His baseball metaphor for announcing his sexual intentions
jokes.explanation: Great metaphor that's both arrogant and funny. Classic Larry confidence mixed with desperation.

jokes.text: I find it a little odd, frankly, that I got you this $300 gift certificate to the restaurant and you chose to take them and not me.
jokes.setup: Larry seeing Ted and Mary at the restaurant he gave them a gift certificate for
jokes.punchline: His audacious expectation to be included in their gift usage
jokes.explanation: Peak Larry entitlement and social obliviousness. Perfect character moment that's painfully relatable.

jokes.text: But if we took you, wouldn't that be you giving a gift to yourself?
jokes.setup: Larry expecting to be included in their restaurant visit
jokes.punchline: Ted's logical takedown of Larry's expectation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant logical response that completely deflates Larry's position. Perfect economy and structure.

jokes.text: At least I got you a gift, okay? I didn't let my daughter go up and sing a song. Is that supposed to be a gift? That is not a gift.
jokes.setup: Argument escalating about gifts and consideration
jokes.punchline: Larry bringing up the singing incident to deflect
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates the conflict. Larry's inability to let things go.

jokes.text: I can't stand the sound of the human voice. My father used to sing in the house all the time. I couldn't take it. And to this day, any singing around me...
jokes.setup: Larry defending his interruption of Sammy's song
jokes.punchline: His ridiculous psychological explanation for hating singing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist justification. Larry's ability to create elaborate explanations for his behavior.

jokes.text: Hey hey hey. Hey hey. That's okay. We've got it. We've got it. We've got it. We're good. Hey. We're good. We're good. Pipe it down. We're good. We're good.
jokes.setup: Live singers performing at the restaurant
jokes.punchline: Larry frantically trying to stop them mid-performance
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to his singing phobia. Peak cringe as he desperately tries to control the situation.

jokes.text: Hey, would you mind switching sides? Switch si... Sides? Uh, okay. I kinda prefer that side.
jokes.setup: Larry and Mary Jane at dinner
jokes.punchline: Larry making an arbitrary and controlling request about seating
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's controlling nature and odd preferences.

jokes.text: Oh, shit. It's my boyfriend. You've got to be quiet. What are you talking... You didn't tell me you had a boyfriend.
jokes.setup: Larry's romantic evening with Mary Jane
jokes.punchline: Sudden revelation that she has a boyfriend
jokes.explanation: Good plot twist that creates immediate tension and conflict.

jokes.text: Oh, god. No no, I'm watching tv. It's like on the discovery channel.
jokes.setup: Mary Jane's boyfriend calling while Larry is there
jokes.punchline: Her terrible excuse for the noises Larry is making
jokes.explanation: Weak excuse that adds to the tension. Discovery Channel is a funny specific choice.

jokes.text: Well, your restaurant recommendation was so awful, I just don't see how I can trust your medical recommendation. They're different. Not to me.
jokes.setup: Doctor giving medical advice about the bandage
jokes.punchline: Larry's illogical connection between restaurant and medical recommendations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic that perfectly captures Larry's irrational thinking patterns. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: I know, I'm sorry. It's crazy, but he just... He just wore me down. It was so horrible, and he's on his way to your house right now.
jokes.setup: Mary Jane calling Larry about her boyfriend finding out
jokes.punchline: She gave the boyfriend Larry's address
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that creates the episode's climactic chase sequence.

jokes.text: You know, you're a doctor. You're sending me out to get hurt. What about the hippocratic oath? When you're hurt, come back and I'll treat you.
jokes.setup: Doctor refusing to let Larry stay
jokes.punchline: Larry's manipulation attempt and doctor's literal interpretation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant use of the Hippocratic oath and the doctor's deadpan response. Great absurdist logic.

jokes.text: Take your $150... and go buy yourself some fucking pants, will you?
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about the $150
jokes.punchline: Susie combining money with pants insult
jokes.explanation: Great callback to pants theme while showing Susie's exasperation

jokes.text: You are a selfish motherfucker. I'm selfish? You are. I buy you a fantastic gift, and you take jeff greene? That's $150. So what? That's my $150!
jokes.setup: Larry asking Susie for sanctuary
jokes.punchline: Their mutual accusations and Larry's focus on the money
jokes.explanation: Good character conflict with callbacks to earlier events. Larry's focus on money is perfect.

jokes.text: Jesus. Shut up! What the fuck? Shut the fuck up! Jesus christ!
jokes.setup: Sammy singing again at Jeff's house
jokes.punchline: Larry's explosive reaction to more singing
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the singing phobia. Larry's complete meltdown is both shocking and inevitable.

jokes.text: You squashed the spirit of a talented young girl! You piece of shit! You heartless piece of shit!
jokes.setup: Larry cursing at Sammy again
jokes.punchline: Susie's escalating insults about Larry's impact on her daughter
jokes.explanation: Susie's protective mother rage escalating to personal attacks

jokes.text: Oh, wait, are you larry david? No. Nuh-uh. No. No. What?
jokes.setup: Larry trying to hide from Mary Jane's boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Stranger recognizing him and Larry's terrible denial
jokes.explanation: Good final twist where Larry's celebrity works against him. His denial is pathetically obvious.

jokes.text: You're not looking for larry david by any chance, are you? Yeah, I am. Thank you. Thanks, man. Hey!
jokes.setup: Larry desperately trying to escape recognition
jokes.punchline: Stranger helpfully directing the boyfriend to Larry
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic ending where trying to escape leads directly to being caught. Good structure for episode close.

tmdb_overview: Larry makes an enemy of Christian Slater at Ted and Mary's anniversary party, but gets his payback while at a restaurant with an old flame. Meanwhile, Larry's doctor gives him his personal number and soon regrets it.
